mn4ma0yyiu5rv.body: Hey bro, so I spoke to my friends about this idea and we exploded and brainstorm and stuff like that, but I have not got an encouraging response at all. Like they are not discouraging me from exploring this and there is generally pretty positive encouraging sort of people, so like I really really didn’t see this as good opportunity at all, so a few things to observe first of all the whole social messaging impact you know in any any any of this kind of messaging people in India, don’t give a shit about. They were like actually the main main. The first and foremost main point was that this whole fashion brand apparel market is a highly competitive, highly crowded and fragmented market. You will either succeed if your large scale general brand or higher and sort of fashion brand on a smaller scale. This thing to stand out is first of all, just generally a very very ambitious thing to do because it’s very it’s. It’s a very competitive fragmented space and getting distribution and visibility. All of these things are extremely difficult. Number two the idea if you have like a very unique creative brand idea and stuff like that, of course that should be the starting point to even jump in and challenge that First challenge so for that like this whole sort of social messaging activism, of course, not activism, but like just generally, you know like kind of socially responsible, environmentally responsible, et cetera, et cetera. People don’t give a shit in India. Nobody cares and so then I kind of debate with my friend that there are a lot of animals lovers and stuff like that, and basically, the point that she gave me, and this is my friend who is into. Sort of sustainable fashion across the world that’s pretty well, she also said the same thing that my other friend had said that you know just because people are animal lovers and they care for cause and stuff like that doesn’t mean they will start sort of dressing up or for for that cause unless it cause very closely associated with the so if you really want to get into this particular cause animal welfare in these adoption, care of dogs and things like that, the best way to do it and this was just a suggestion. She was like get closer to these communities and groups and stuff and really try to understand what the problem is and try to sort figure out what kind of products and services actually cater to a need that exist. This is more like you know, just manufacturing a solution to a problem, that doesn’t exist so the whole fashion thing and things like that don’t quite it. Just doesn’t tool. Don’t add up for them. There is no business sense in the thing, and there is no real creative, which people would buy into in terms of dying these two together.

Bill management system. 
So different sources:
- online payments
- online subscriptions
- live real-time paper bills, etc.
- salaries and other periodic expenses


Next - Some parameters could be:
- fixed versus variable pricing
- frequency (weekly, monthly, yearly, or other)
 Subscription versus one-time payments 
- payment mode; auto??
- bill track, automate, reminders, 
Some additional stuff like:
- categorization
- personal
- professional
- communication (like phone, internet, etc.)


Then entertainment, which would be all your OTT subscriptions and things like that. Then utilities like:
- water
- gas
- electricity
Then lifestyle like credit cards and things like that. Category is one ...salaries as well

Then there's other expense tracking also like groceries and toiletries and things like that, but that's an expense management system. Right now we're talking about bill payment and subscription management and stuff. That can be phase two

mqnj9lrxnjj0g.body:
**Smoking Journal**
28th June - 5 Days FREE !!
- comfortable smoking bangs very minor and laughable. One drink night. Home. Smooth
- feels so light so clean. No trap. No yuckiness. Free of so much shit
- there’s been a lot of overheating, over aggrso…overcoming now. Forever!
- unwell today. Remember just how horrible being unwell feels. 


- Indulgent weekend — Friday night at Ashish's was crazy.
- Tried to quit Saturday, caved at night — told myself "a few smokes, back on track Sunday."
- Sunday cravings overwhelming, gave myself another day.

One smoke → immediate regret, restlessness, feeling bad. Then no relief — craving more, feeling worse than before I smoked. The cravings aren't even being relieved. Now I'm carrying the full weight of actually smoking, which is worse than bearing the cravings — and the cravings were temporary.
- smoked two cigs so far and ready feeling real shitty!!

FREEDOM DAY 2 
- anger : nicotine monster dying. We’re free!
- smoking feels like PUNISHMENT. so hard to even get through a cig. 
- chest tight, throat shut, everything yuck, nothing to gain, so much suffering
- smoking craving — complete illusion!

mrjj73ru19qcj.body:
- for creative things, the more specific i am the better! dont expect it to come up with things..you may or may not like it...but if you tell it exactly what and how you want it, it should build it. 
each stop will have engagement/feedback mechanims (eg. like, useful, etc. system learns preferences)
- while navigating the voyage, templated VIDEOS are read!, experienced at arrival or saved for later
- In future, there can be satsangs, virtual retreats, book readings etc. 
--> fundamentally, - it's never about the teachings, *it's about my problem* and arriving at a solution, practice etc. along the way, i LEARN new insights and i'm wowed by the experience; and surprised at the wisdom and UNITY
